[Text-to-SQL Benchmarks and the Current State-of-the-Art | by Ainesh Pandey | Dataherald | Medium](https://medium.com/dataherald/text-to-sql-benchmarks-and-the-current-state-of-the-art-63dd3b3943fe)\n4. [[1809.08887] Spider: A Large-Scale Human-Labeled Dataset for Complex and Cross-Domain Semantic Parsing and Text-to-SQL Task](https://arxiv.org/abs/1809.08887)\n5. [](https://aclanthology.org/D18-1425.pdf)\n\n# Spider Dataset NL2SQL Statistics\n\nThe Spider dataset is a large-scale semantic parsing and text-to-SQL benchmark with the following key statistics:[1][4]\n\n**Dataset Size:**\n- **10,181 questions** paired with natural language queries[1][4]\n- **5,693 unique complex SQL queries**[1][4]\n- **200 databases** with multiple tables[1][4]\n- **138 different domains** covered[1][4]\n\n**Key Characteristics:**\nSpider is designed as a complex, cross-domain dataset where different SQL queries and database schemas appear in the training and test sets, requiring models to generalize to both new queries and new database schemas.[1][4][5]\n\n**Annotation:**\nThe dataset was annotated by 11 Yale students and includes complex SQL operations involving joins, subqueries, and aggregations across multiple tables.[1][5]\n\n**Related Dataset:**\nSpider 2.0, a more realistic and challenging version, was released in November 2024 as an updated benchmark for the text-to-SQL task.[1]",
